name: Interleaved Renderer

tasks.title: Implement interleaved renderer
tasks.body: Create internal/logrender/interleaved.go. Implement an InterleavedRenderer struct that accepts an io.Writer. The Render(ctx context.Context, records <-chan Record) method processes records chronologically and produces interleaved output. Stage events (stage_start, stage_complete, planning_start, planning_complete) print as timestamped header lines: 'HH:MM:SS ▶ [stage-label] node-address' for starts, 'HH:MM:SS ✓ [stage-label] node-address (duration)' for completions. The timestamp is the record's wall-clock time formatted in local timezone. Assistant records print indented with a timestamp: 'HH:MM:SS     text'. The 4-space indent visually separates thoughts from stage headers per the spec. audit_report_written records print indented below the audit completion. Duration calculation requires tracking stage_start timestamps the same way the summary renderer does. The renderer must buffer start times keyed by (stage, node, task). Skip terminal_marker, daemon_start, daemon_stop, and other non-display record types. Works for both replay and follow since it processes records as they arrive.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Interleaved Renderer is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify the 4-space indent on assistant text lines and HH:MM:SS timestamp format in local timezone

breadcrumbs.text: Implemented InterleavedRenderer in internal/logrender/interleaved.go with 15 tests at 100% coverage. Handles stage_start/complete, planning_start/complete, assistant, and audit_report_written records. Filters daemon stages and irrelevant types. Timestamps formatted as HH:MM:SS in local timezone.

audit.result_summary: InterleavedRenderer implemented with full test coverage. Processes records chronologically, printing timestamped stage headers (with ▶/✓/✗ glyphs and durations) interleaved with 4-space-indented assistant thoughts and audit report paths. Reuses existing package helpers for stage keys, duration formatting, and glyph selection. Single Render method works for both replay and follow modes.

task-0001.objective: Implement the InterleavedRenderer that combines stage headers and agent thoughts with wall-clock timestamps
task-0001.what_happened: Created internal/logrender/interleaved.go with InterleavedRenderer struct. Single Render method handles both replay and follow modes by processing records as they arrive. Uses the same stageKey/skipStage/glyphFor/nodeAddress helpers as SummaryRenderer. Added formatTimestamp helper for HH:MM:SS local-time formatting. Assistant text indented 4 spaces, audit reports indented the same way.
went_well: Clean reuse of existing helpers (stageKey, keyFor, skipStage, glyphFor, nodeAddress, FormatDuration) kept the implementation tight at ~100 lines
Test suite uses a localTS helper to compute expected timestamps from UTC inputs, making tests pass in any timezone
improvements: The formatTimestamp function is private to this file but could be shared if other renderers need timestamps later
action_items: Wire InterleavedRenderer into the log command's --interleaved flag handler
